Sources Outbreaks specifically caused by serogroup A, C, W, or Y meningococcal disease. 
Outbreaks specifically caused by serogroup B meningococcal disease. 
Meningococcal 
Meningococcal disease is an uncommon but serious illness that causes meningitis and bloodstream infections. It can be deadly, but vaccines help prevent it.
